items.title: Restoring a Trashed Star Trek Control Panel
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content: <article>After months of delays and procrastination, Mike, Jay and Rich Evans decide to finally attempt to do something with the Deep Space Nine USS Defiant Helm Control Panel they purchased from Propstore. It was cracked in half. Scratched, ripped, warped and all sorts of bad. Plus it needed to be lit. So the gang attempts their unique style of drunken midwestern problem solving. See the results! See the horror! Witness the shame! But seriously. Mike emailed with Michael Okuda and got some info on the prop. The theory is that this is the panel used on the show from the first appearance of the Defiant until the season 6 episode "One Little Ship" where a stunt explosions appears to happen right at the split mark. Maybe it was just a crack in the plexiglass, but enough to cause them to replace it because the next time we see the bridge is on the "Valiant" (same set) and there is a new LCARS layout. This is a theory. Hopefully someone can shed some light on this, but alas it is what it is. A pile of junk lovingly restored to something we can display and that warms our cold, dead hearts.</article>

items.title: When an Actor Saves His Career and Life - Colin Farrell
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>Colin Farrell is now one of Hollywood’s most respected character actors, delivering unforgettable performances in The Penguin, The Banshees of Inisherin, and The Lobster. But two decades ago, he was being pushed as Hollywood’s next leading man with roles in Minority Report, Daredevil, and Phone Booth. His quick rise brought fame and money, but it also worsened his addictions. Soon, his bad-boy image became very public, and he couldn’t even remember filming entire movies. Hollywood began to write him off.

But Farrell’s comeback isn’t just about reviving his career, but also about reinventing himself as a person. From rock bottom to career-defining performances, Farrell’s journey is about growth, redemption, and defying expectations. Today, we’re diving into the rise, fall, and revival of Colin Farrell. We’ll explore his battles with addiction and fame, what finally pushed him to change, and how he transformed from Hollywood’s wildcard to one of its most respected actors. This is the story of an actor who saved his career, and himself. This is Colin Farrell.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
01:03 - Rise & Early Struggles
05:25 - His Lowest Point
09:46 - A Comeback?
13:54 - Found His Place

Colin Farrell. In Bruges. The Penguin. The Batman. The Banshees of Inisherin. The Lobster. Angelman Syndrome Son. Minority Report. Steven Spielberg. Daredevil. Bullseye. Phone Booth. Joel Schumacher. Tigerland. Tom Cruise. Hart's War. Britney Spears. The Recruit. Alexander. Biggest Box Office Bomb. Miami Vice. Terrence Malick. The New World. Crazy Heart. The Imaginarium of Doctor Parnassus. Horrible Bosses. Fright Night. Seven Psychopaths. Martin McDonagh. Yorgos Lanthimos. True Detective. Fantastic Beasts. Widows. The Gentlemen. The North Water. After Yang. Thirteen Lives. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.

Patreon: https://patreon.com/FilmStack
Twitter: https://twitter.com/realFilmStack
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/

#colinfarrell    #dedication    #bestactor</article>

items.title: ANOTHER Gremlins 3 Pitch!!!
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content:
<article>In Joe Dante's spirit of anarchy and self-aware commentary on sequels that was Gremlins 2, it's only natural to do that again! But bigger!! This time with the ghoulish things that are soulless corporate reboots. 

A real life Gizmo lives in Joe Dante's basement and REAL Gremlins are unleashed on the set of the pathetic "Gremlins” soft reboot. A lazy director-for-hire, a moronic studio producer, and clueless actors suffer the consequences as Zach Galligan intentionally sabotages the film. After realizing the mistake he’s made, Zach and his co-star son Finn Wolfhard must stop the Gremlins before the escape the studio lot, or worse knock down the famed Warner Brothers water tower! 

Some notes for after viewing:

Of course Stripe is hiding inside a Gizmo costume. Makes much more sense.
And Phoebe Cates will return to acting as she'll be the one who plays Snow White in the fake film reboot trap. That's an obvious one we didn't think of. 

Thanks for listening!!!</article>
